Title: A novel C2H2‐type zinc‐finger transcription factor, CitZAT4, regulates ethylene‐induced orange coloration in Satsuma mandarin flavedo (Citrus unshiu Marc.)

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ethylene-induced in flavedo during postharvest storage, especially at 5 DAT
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: orange peel coloration and fruit quality/economic value
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: higher CitPSY expression increases phytoene and supports increased total and beta-branch carotenoids during ethylene-induced orange coloration
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ethephon/ethylene treatment; blocked by 1-MCP; transient overexpression or RNAi of CitZAT4 under ethylene context
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful downstream biosynthetic target for engineering stronger carotenoid flux together with CitZAT4/CitERF061 to enhance orange coloration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling under ethylene/1-MCP; correlation with metabolite profiling; promoter binding and activation by CitZAT4 via Y1H, ChIP-qPCR, EMSA, dual-LUC; expression changes in CitZAT4 overexpression/RNAi tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified here mainly as a direct target of CitZAT4 and as the source of increased phytoene substrate for downstream branch flux.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ethylene induced CitPSY expression in flavedo. 2. CitZAT4 directly bound and activated the CitPSY promoter. 3. Increased CitPSY expression was associated with elevated phytoene and enhanced downstream orange carotenoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native CitPSY promoter for Y1H/ChIP/EMSA/LUC; 35S-driven CitZAT4 effector in dual-LUC
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: ethylene-induced in flavedo during postharvest storage

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: C2H2-type zinc-finger transcription factor with three C2H2 zinc-finger domains, EAR motif, and C-terminal transactivation region
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: strongly induced by ethylene in flavedo, especially at 5 DAT
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: strongly induced by ethylene in flavedo; overexpressed in citrus calli and transiently in fruit
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: beta-cryptoxanthin; beta-carotene; violaxanthin; lutein; total carotenoids
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising target to improve citrus peel orange coloration and carotenoid composition by promoting beta-branch flux; could be stacked with CitERF061 or edited for stronger ethylene responsiveness
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ethephon/ethylene treatment; effect blocked by 1-MCP; ACC enhanced promoter activation in dual-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: direct transcriptional activation of CitPSY, CitLCYB, CitHYD, and CitNCED2; direct transcriptional repression of CitLCYE
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CitZAT4 is an ethylene-induced nuclear C2H2 zinc-finger TF essential for orange flavedo formation. 2. It directly binds promoters of CitPSY, CitLCYB, CitHYD, CitNCED2, and CitLCYE. 3. It activates upstream and beta-branch genes but represses alpha-branch CitLCYE, shifting carotenoid flux from alpha- to beta-branch and increasing orange carotenoids, especially beta-cryptoxanthin.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: overexpression made calli more yellow/orange-like due to carotenoid changes; no broader growth penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PH7-CitZAT4 and PK7-CitZAT4 overexpression constructs; RNAi-CitZAT4; Pro35S:CitZAT4 effector in dual-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: subcellular localization; Y2H transactivation mapping; qRT-PCR; stable overexpression and RNAi in citrus calli; transient overexpression and RNAi in fruit; HPLC metabolite profiling; Y1H; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although containing an EAR repression motif, CitZAT4 showed dual regulatory activity, acting as activator for several carotenogenic genes and repressor for CitLCYE.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates CitPSY, CitLCYB, CitHYD, and CitNCED2; represses CitLCYE
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes orange coloration by increasing beta-branch orange carotenoids and decreasing alpha-branch lutein
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential ethylene-responsive switch controlling carotenoid metabolic flux into the beta-branch in flavedo
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: carotenoid biosynthesis branch selection and pathway flux reprogramming
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ethylene signaling; upstream activation by CitERF061

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: ethylene-induced; overexpression in calli and fruit increased CitZAT4 expression and carotenoids; endogenous expression in wild-type calli appeared very low
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ethylene-induced regulator acting upstream of CitZAT4
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful upstream regulatory target for enhancing citrus carotenoid accumulation and peel coloration, potentially in combination with CitZAT4
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ethylene/ethephon treatment; ACC enhanced activation of CitZAT4 promoter in dual-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CitERF061 directly binds the CitZAT4 promoter and activates its transcription. 2. Ethylene strengthens this activation, placing CitERF061 upstream of CitZAT4. 3. Overexpression of CitERF061 increases CitZAT4 expression, carotenoid accumulation, and orange coloration.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi effect in calli was weak, likely because endogenous expression was very low; no broader growth penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PH7-CitERF061 and PK7-CitERF061 overexpression constructs; RNAi-CitERF061; CitZAT4 promoter reporter in dual-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; dual-luciferase assay; stable overexpression and RNAi in citrus calli; transient fruit expression; qRT-PCR; HPLC metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previous work showed ERF061 activates nine beta-branch carotenogenic genes; in this paper its key core role is as direct upstream activator of CitZAT4 in an ethylene-driven cascade.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively promotes orange coloration and carotenoid accumulation through activation of CitZAT4
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: ethylene-responsive upstream activator that initiates the CitZAT4 transcriptional cascade linked to orange carotenoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ethylene-responsive transcriptional control of carotenoid biosynthesis via upstream cascade
